content.text:
<user_query>
Create a new Python project called backtrace-agent. This is the first module of an autonomous incident-response agent — a long-running service that watches application logs, detects new error fingerprints, and (in later modules) opens fix PRs. For Module 1, you are building only the skeleton: Docker setup, FastAPI shell with a health check, structured logging, Postgres wired up via docker-compose, and the dev tooling. No business logic yet.
Tech stack:

Python 3.12
FastAPI (with uvicorn[standard] as the ASGI server)
PostgreSQL 16 (via docker-compose, NOT installed in the agent image)
pydantic-settings for config
structlog for structured JSON logging
pytest + pytest-asyncio + httpx for tests
Dependency management via pyproject.toml with uv (or pip if uv is unavailable — pick uv if possible, it's significantly faster)

Repo layout to create:

├── docker-compose.yml
├── Dockerfile
├── .dockerignore
├── .gitignore
├── .env.example
├── pyproject.toml
├── README.md
├── src/
│   └── backtrace_agent/
│       ├── __init__.py
│       ├── main.py
│       ├── config.py
│       ├── logging_setup.py
│       └── api/
│           ├── __init__.py
│           └── health.py
└── tests/
    ├── __init__.py
    ├── conftest.py
    └── test_health.py
pyproject.toml: Use a modern PEP 621 layout. Package name backtrace-agent, version 0.1.0. Dependencies: fastapi, uvicorn[standard], pydantic, pydantic-settings, structlog, python-json-logger. Dev dependencies (under [project.optional-dependencies] dev): pytest, pytest-asyncio, httpx, ruff. Set the src/ layout under [tool.hatchline.build.targets.wheel] or equivalent — use hatchling as the build backend. Configure ruff with line-length = 100 and select rules E, F, I, W, UP. Configure pytest to discover under tests/ with asyncio_mode = "auto".
src/backtrace_agent/config.py: A Settings class extending pydantic_settings.BaseSettings. Fields (all with sensible defaults):

app_name: str = "backtrace-agent"
environment: str = "development"
log_level: str = "INFO"
database_url: str = "postgresql+asyncpg://backtrace:backtrace@localhost:5432/backtrace"
api_host: str = "0.0.0.0"
api_port: int = 8000

Configure model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_file=".env", env_file_encoding="utf-8", extra="ignore"). Export a module-level settings = Settings() instance.
src/backtrace_agent/logging_setup.py: A configure_logging(log_level: str) function that sets up structlog to emit single-line JSON to stdout. Each log line must include at minimum: timestamp (ISO 8601 with Z suffix), level, event (the log message), logger (the module name), service ("backtrace-agent"). Use structlog.processors.TimeStamper(fmt="iso", utc=True). Use structlog.processors.JSONRenderer() as the final processor. Wire it so standard library logging (which uvicorn and FastAPI use) also routes through structlog — use structlog.stdlib.ProcessorFormatter to bridge.
src/backtrace_agent/api/health.py: A FastAPI APIRouter with one route: GET /healthz returning {"status": "ok", "service": "backtrace-agent"} with HTTP 200. Add type hints.
src/backtrace_agent/main.py:

Import settings, configure logging at module import time
Create a FastAPI app with title="Backtrace Agent", version="0.1.0"
Use a lifespan async context manager (not deprecated @app.on_event). On startup, log an INFO event agent.starting including environment and log_level. On shutdown, log agent.stopping.
Include the health router
Expose a module-level app variable for uvicorn to find

Dockerfile: Multi-stage build.
Stage 1 (builder):

Base: python:3.12-slim
Install uv via pip install uv (or use the official ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv image as the builder if you prefer)
Set workdir /app
Copy pyproject.toml and any lock file
Run uv pip install --system --no-cache . to install dependencies into the system site-packages (no venv inside container — keeps things simple)

Stage 2 (runtime):

Base: python:3.12-slim
Create a non-root user agent with UID 1000
Copy installed packages from the builder stage's /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages and /usr/local/bin
Set workdir /app
Copy the src/ directory with correct ownership
Set PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1, PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1, PYTHONPATH=/app/src
EXPOSE 8000
Switch to the agent user
CMD ["uvicorn", "backtrace_agent.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]

The final image must NOT contain build tools, dev dependencies, or test files.
.dockerignore: Exclude .git, .venv, __pycache__, *.pyc, .pytest_cache, .ruff_cache, tests/, .env, *.log, logs/, README.md, docker-compose.yml.
.gitignore: Include .venv/, __pycache__/, *.pyc, .pytest_cache/, .ruff_cache/, .env, *.log, logs/, .coverage, dist/, build/, *.egg-info/, .DS_Store.
.env.example: A template with all settings keys from Settings, with the same defaults, and comments explaining each. Mark it clearly as a template that should be copied to .env.
docker-compose.yml: Use the Compose v3.8 spec. Two services:

postgres:

Image: postgres:16-alpine
Environment: POSTGRES_USER=backtrace, POSTGRES_PASSWORD=backtrace, POSTGRES_DB=backtrace
Port: 5432:5432
Volume: a named volume postgres_data mounted at /var/lib/postgresql/data
Healthcheck: pg_isready -U backtrace, interval 5s, retries 5

agent:

Build: . with the Dockerfile
depends_on: postgres with condition service_healthy
Environment: read from .env file, plus override DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://backtrace:backtrace@postgres:5432/backtrace (note: hostname is postgres, the service name, not localhost)
Port: 8000:8000
Restart policy: unless-stopped

Declare the postgres_data named volume at the bottom.
tests/conftest.py: Provide a pytest fixture client that yields an httpx.AsyncClient wrapped around the FastAPI app using ASGITransport. Use pytest-asyncio so the fixture is async.
tests/test_health.py: One async test: test_healthz_returns_ok that calls GET /healthz via the client, asserts status 200, asserts the JSON body equals {"status": "ok", "service": "backtrace-agent"}.
README.md: Document four things, concisely:

What this project is (one paragraph — incident response agent, Phase 1 module 1)
Local development without Docker: how to install with uv pip install -e ".[dev]", run with uvicorn backtrace_agent.main:app --reload, test with pytest
Running via Docker: cp .env.example .env, docker compose up --build, then curl localhost:8000/healthz
The fact that this is Module 1 of N — only health check works for now, real functionality lands in later modules

Hard constraints:

No business logic. No detection, no fingerprinting, no DB queries beyond what's needed to verify the connection works (and for Module 1, that's none — DB is just provisioned, not yet used by code).
All Python files under 100 lines. Module 1 is a skeleton; if a file is getting long, it's too clever.
Type hints on every function signature.
No print() calls anywhere — all output via structlog.
The container must run as a non-root user.
The image must build successfully with docker compose build and bring up both services with docker compose up.
The health check at http://localhost:8000/healthz must return 200 within 5 seconds of docker compose up completing
</user_query>

Creating the backtrace-agent skeleton: checking the workspace, then adding the project files.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Check if uv is available

content.text:
Creating the full project structure in the empty workspace.

[REDACTED]

input.contents:
[project]
name = "backtrace-agent"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Autonomous incident-response agent — log watching and error fingerprint detection"
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.12"
dependencies = [
    "fastapi",
    "uvicorn[standard]",
    "pydantic",
    "pydantic-settings",
    "structlog",
    "python-json-logger",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pytest",
    "pytest-asyncio",
    "httpx",
    "ruff",
]

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["src/backtrace_agent"]

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 100

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = ["E", "F", "I", "W", "UP"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
asyncio_mode = "auto"

"""Application configuration via environment variables."""

from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    """Runtime settings loaded from environment and optional .env file."""

    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
        env_file=".env",
        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
        extra="ignore",
    )

    app_name: str = "backtrace-agent"
    environment: str = "development"
    log_level: str = "INFO"
    database_url: str = "postgresql+asyncpg://backtrace:backtrace@localhost:5432/backtrace"
    api_host: str = "0.0.0.0"
    api_port: int = 8000


settings = Settings()

"""Structured JSON logging via structlog with stdlib bridge."""

import logging
import sys

import structlog
from structlog.processors import JSONRenderer, TimeStamper
from structlog.stdlib import ProcessorFormatter


def _add_service(_logger: object, _method: str, event_dict: dict) -> dict:
    event_dict.setdefault("service", "backtrace-agent")
    return event_dict


def configure_logging(log_level: str) -> None:
    """Configure structlog and stdlib logging to emit single-line JSON to stdout."""
    timestamper = TimeStamper(fmt="iso", utc=True)

    shared_processors: list[structlog.types.Processor] = [
        structlog.contextvars.merge_contextvars,
        structlog.stdlib.add_log_level,
        structlog.stdlib.add_logger_name,
        _add_service,
        timestamper,
        structlog.processors.StackInfoRenderer(),
        structlog.processors.format_exc_info,
        structlog.processors.UnicodeDecoder(),
    ]

    structlog.configure(
        processors=[
            structlog.stdlib.filter_by_level,
            *shared_processors,
            ProcessorFormatter.wrap_for_formatter,
        ],
        logger_factory=structlog.stdlib.LoggerFactory(),
        wrapper_class=structlog.stdlib.BoundLogger,
        cache_logger_on_first_use=True,
    )

    formatter = ProcessorFormatter(
        foreign_pre_chain=shared_processors,
        processors=[
            ProcessorFormatter.remove_processors_meta,
            JSONRenderer(),
        ],
    )

    handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
    handler.setFormatter(formatter)

    root = logging.getLogger()
    root.handlers.clear()
    root.addHandler(handler)
    root.setLevel(log_level.upper())

"""Health check endpoint."""

from fastapi import APIRouter
from pydantic import BaseModel

router = APIRouter(tags=["health"])


class HealthResponse(BaseModel):
    status: str
    service: str


@router.get("/healthz", response_model=HealthResponse)
async def healthz() -> HealthResponse:
    """Liveness probe — confirms the service process is running."""
    return HealthResponse(status="ok", service="backtrace-agent")

"""FastAPI application entrypoint."""

from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from typing import AsyncIterator

import structlog
from fastapi import FastAPI

from backtrace_agent.api.health import router as health_router
from backtrace_agent.config import settings
from backtrace_agent.logging_setup import configure_logging

configure_logging(settings.log_level)
logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__)


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(_app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:
    logger.info(
        "agent.starting",
        environment=settings.environment,
        log_level=settings.log_level,
    )
    yield
    logger.info("agent.stopping")


app = FastAPI(title="Backtrace Agent", version="0.1.0", lifespan=lifespan)
app.include_router(health_router)

"""Pytest fixtures."""

from collections.abc import AsyncIterator

import pytest
from httpx import ASGITransport, AsyncClient

from backtrace_agent.main import app


@pytest.fixture
async def client() -> AsyncIterator[AsyncClient]:
    transport = ASGITransport(app=app)
    async with AsyncClient(transport=transport, base_url="http://test") as ac:
        yield ac

"""Health endpoint tests."""

import pytest
from httpx import AsyncClient


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_healthz_returns_ok(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    response = await client.get("/healthz")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json() == {"status": "ok", "service": "backtrace-agent"}

# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1

FROM python:3.12-slim AS builder

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir uv

WORKDIR /app

COPY pyproject.toml .
COPY src/ src/

RUN uv pip install --system --no-cache .

FROM python:3.12-slim AS runtime

RUN groupadd --gid 1000 agent \
    && useradd --uid 1000 --gid 1000 --create-home --shell /usr/sbin/nologin agent

COPY --from=builder /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages
COPY --from=builder /usr/local/bin /usr/local/bin

WORKDIR /app

COPY --chown=agent:agent src/ src/

ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONPATH=/app/src

EXPOSE 8000

USER agent

CMD ["uvicorn", "backtrace_agent.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]

# Template — copy this file to .env and adjust values for your environment.
#   cp .env.example .env

# Application display name (used in logs and metadata).
APP_NAME=backtrace-agent

# Deployment environment: development, staging, production.
ENVIRONMENT=development

# Logging verbosity: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR.
LOG_LEVEL=INFO

# Async SQLAlchemy-style database URL (Postgres via asyncpg driver).
# In docker-compose the agent service overrides this to use hostname "postgres".
DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://backtrace:backtrace@localhost:5432/backtrace

# Uvicorn bind address (inside the container use 0.0.0.0).
API_HOST=0.0.0.0

# Uvicorn listen port.
API_PORT=8000

version: "3.8"

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: backtrace
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: backtrace
      POSTGRES_DB: backtrace
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U backtrace"]
      interval: 5s
      retries: 5

  agent:
    build: .
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+asyncpg://backtrace:backtrace@postgres:5432/backtrace
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  postgres_data:

# backtrace-agent

Autonomous incident-response agent that watches application logs, detects new error fingerprints, and (in later phases) opens fix pull requests. **Module 1** is the service skeleton: FastAPI health check, structured logging, Postgres provisioned via Docker Compose, and dev tooling — no detection or remediation logic yet.

## Local development (without Docker)

```bash
uv pip install -e ".[dev]"
uvicorn backtrace_agent.main:app --reload
pytest
```

Set `PYTHONPATH=src` if the package is not installed, or use editable install as above.

## Docker

```bash
cp .env.example .env
docker compose up --build
curl http://localhost:8000/healthz
```

## Roadmap

This repository is **Module 1 of N**. Only `/healthz` is implemented; log ingestion, fingerprinting, and PR automation arrive in later modules.

content.text:
The **backtrace-agent** skeleton is in place under `e:\Trilogy\Backtrace_TI`. Here is what was created and verified.

### Layout
All requested files are present: `pyproject.toml` (hatchling, ruff, pytest), `src/backtrace_agent/` (config, logging, FastAPI app, `/healthz`), `tests/`, Docker/Compose files, `.env.example`, and `README.md`.

### Verified locally
- `uv venv` + `uv pip install -e ".[dev]"` — installs successfully  
- `pytest` — 1 test passed (`test_healthz_returns_ok`)  
- `ruff check` — clean  

### Docker
`docker compose build` failed because **Docker Desktop is not running** on this machine (`dockerDesktopLinuxEngine` pipe missing). After starting Docker:

```powershell
cd e:\Trilogy\Backtrace_TI
docker compose up --build
curl http://localhost:8000/healthz
```

A `.env` was copied from `.env.example` for Compose; adjust it as needed.

### Highlights
| Area | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| **Config** | `Settings` in `config.py` with `.env` loading and `settings` singleton |
| **Logging** | JSON to stdout via structlog + stdlib bridge; fields include timestamp (ISO UTC), level, event, logger, service |
| **API** | `GET /healthz` → `{"status": "ok", "service": "backtrace-agent"}` |
| **Lifespan** | Logs `agent.starting` / `agent.stopping` on startup/shutdown |
| **Image** | Multi-stage build, non-root `agent` (UID 1000), no tests/dev tools in runtime |
| **Postgres** | `postgres:16-alpine` with healthcheck; agent waits for healthy DB |

### Quick start (no Docker)
```powershell
cd e:\Trilogy\Backtrace_TI
uv venv
.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
uv pip install -e ".[dev]"
uvicorn backtrace_agent.main:app --reload
```

This is **Module 1 of N** — only the health check and infrastructure are wired; log detection and DB usage come in later modules.

content.text:
Docker isn’t reachable from this Cursor agent shell — `docker version` only shows the **Client** block and errors on `dockerDesktopLinuxEngine`. That usually means the engine isn’t fully up yet, or this terminal can’t access Docker’s named pipe. Run the commands below in **your own** PowerShell (outside the agent) once Docker Desktop shows **Engine running**.

---

## Step-by-step: run with Docker (recommended)

**1. Open PowerShell** and go to the project:

```powershell
cd e:\Trilogy\Backtrace_TI
```

**2. Confirm Docker is ready** (you must see both Client and Server):

```powershell
docker version
```

If you only see Client and an error, wait until Docker Desktop says **Engine running**, then retry.

**3. Create environment file** (skip if `.env` already exists):

```powershell
Copy-Item .env.example .env
```

**4. Build and start** (Postgres + agent):

```powershell
docker compose up --build
```

Leave this running. First run pulls images and builds the agent — may take a few minutes.

**5. In a second PowerShell window**, check health:

```powershell
curl http://localhost:8000/healthz
```

Expected:

```json
{"status":"ok","service":"backtrace-agent"}
```

**6. Optional — run in background:**

```powershell
docker compose up --build -d
docker compose logs -f agent
```

**7. Stop everything:**

```powershell
docker compose down
```

Remove Postgres data too:

```powershell
docker compose down -v
```

---

## Step-by-step: local dev (no Docker)

**1. Prerequisites:** Python 3.12 and [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) installed.

**2. Setup:**

```powershell
cd e:\Trilogy\Backtrace_TI
uv venv
.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
uv pip install -e ".[dev]"
Copy-Item .env.example .env
```

**3. Run the API:**

```powershell
uvicorn backtrace_agent.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
```

**4. Test** (another terminal):

```powershell
curl http://localhost:8000/healthz
pytest
```

Postgres is only required for later modules; Module 1 doesn’t use the DB yet.

---

## Quick reference

| Action | Command |
|--------|---------|
| Build images | `docker compose build` |
| Start stack | `docker compose up --build` |
| Health check | `curl http://localhost:8000/healthz` |
| View logs | `docker compose logs -f` |
| Stop | `docker compose down` |

---

## If Docker still fails on your machine

1. Docker Desktop → wait until status is **Engine running** (green).
2. **Settings → General** → ensure “Use the WSL 2 based engine” is on if you use WSL.
3. Restart Docker Desktop, then run `docker version` again until **Server** appears.
4. Run `docker compose up --build` from a normal PowerShell window (not only from Cursor’s agent terminal).

After `docker compose up` succeeds, paste the output of `curl http://localhost:8000/healthz` (or any error) and we can debug further.

[REDACTED]

time="2026-05-28T17:02:00+05:30" level=warning msg="E:\\Trilogy\\Backtrace_TI\\docker-compose.yml: the attribute `version` is obsolete, it will be ignored, please remove it to avoid potential confusion"
unable to get image 'backtrace_ti-agent': error during connect: Get "http://%2F%2F.%2Fpipe%2FdockerDesktopLinuxEngine/v1.51/images/backtrace_ti-agent/json": open //./pipe/dockerDesktopLinuxEngine: The system cannot find the file specified.

($i/24)"; Start-Sleep -Seconds 5 }; if ($ready) { docker version } else { Write-Host "Engine not ready after 2 minutes" }

    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:

    ports:

  agent:
    build: .
    depends_on:

        condition: service_healthy
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+asyncpg://backtrace:backtrace@postgres:5432/backtrace

[REDACTED]

input.old_string:

COPY src/ src/

RUN uv pip install --system --no-cache .
input.new_string:

COPY src/ src/

# README is in .dockerignore; hatchling needs it at build time only.
RUN printf '%s\n' '# backtrace-agent' > README.md

RUN uv pip install --system --no-cache .

content.text:
Here’s what was going wrong and what we fixed.

## Root cause of your error

This line is the real problem:

```text
open //./pipe/dockerDesktopLinuxEngine: The system cannot find the file specified
```

That means **the Docker engine was not running** — only the Docker CLI was installed. Docker Desktop was closed or still starting, so Compose could not talk to the daemon. It is **not** a bug in `backtrace-agent` itself.

**Fix:** Start **Docker Desktop** from the Start menu and wait until the tray icon shows **Engine running** (often 30–60 seconds). Then verify:

```powershell
docker version
```

You must see both **Client** and **Server** sections. If you only see Client, Docker is not ready yet.

---

## What we fixed in the project

1. **`docker-compose.yml`** — Removed obsolete `version:` (warning gone) and set project name `backtrace`.
2. **`Dockerfile`** — Build failed because `README.md` is in `.dockerignore` but hatchling needs it at build time; added a stub README in the builder stage.

---

## Verified working

The stack is up and healthy:

- `backtrace-postgres-1` — healthy  
- `backtrace-agent-1` — running on port 8000  
- `GET /healthz` — **200 OK**

---

## Commands to run (every time)

### 1. Start Docker Desktop

Open **Docker Desktop** → wait for **Engine running**.

### 2. Confirm Docker works

```powershell
docker version
```

### 3. Go to the project

```powershell
cd E:\Trilogy\Backtrace_TI
```

### 4. Environment file (first time only)

```powershell
Copy-Item .env.example .env
```

### 5. Build and start

**Foreground** (logs in this window):

```powershell
docker compose up --build
```

**Background** (detached):

```powershell
docker compose up --build -d
```

### 6. Health check

```powershell
Invoke-RestMethod http://localhost:8000/healthz
```

Expected:

```json
{"status":"ok","service":"backtrace-agent"}
```

Or:

```powershell
curl.exe http://localhost:8000/healthz
```

Use `curl.exe` on Windows if `curl` hangs (PowerShell alias issue).

### 7. Useful commands

```powershell
docker compose ps              # container status
docker compose logs -f agent   # follow agent logs
docker compose down            # stop everything
docker compose down -v         # stop + delete DB volume
```

---

## If the pipe error comes back

1. Quit Docker Desktop (right‑click tray → **Quit**).
2. Start **Docker Desktop** again.
3. Wait until **Engine running**.
4. Run `docker version` again, then `docker compose up --build`.

If it still fails:

- **Settings → General** → enable **Use the WSL 2 based engine**
- Restart Docker Desktop or reboot Windows
- Run: `wsl --update` in an admin PowerShell

---

Your next step in PowerShell:

```powershell
docker version
docker compose up --build
```

In another window:

```powershell
Invoke-RestMethod http://localhost:8000/healthz
```

**Task result:** The Docker issue was resolved. The `dockerDesktopLinuxEngine` error meant the Docker **engine wasn’t running** — Docker Desktop had to be started and reach “Engine running” before `docker compose` could work.

We also fixed a **build failure** (hatchling needed `README.md` during the image build) and cleaned up `docker-compose.yml`.

The stack was brought up successfully: Postgres healthy, agent on port 8000, and `/healthz` returned **200** with `{"status":"ok","service":"backtrace-agent"}`.

**To run it again:**

```powershell
# 1. Start Docker Desktop and wait for "Engine running"
docker version   # must show Client + Server

cd E:\Trilogy\Backtrace_TI
docker compose up --build -d
Invoke-RestMethod http://localhost:8000/healthz
```

The agent container references `/app/entrypoint.sh`, which isn't in the image. Checking the Docker setup and fixing it.

input.description: Check entrypoint.sh for CRLF bytes
Read first bytes of entrypoint.sh for CRLF

content.text:
CRLF line endings in `entrypoint.sh` (Windows) make Linux look for `/bin/sh\r`, which doesn't exist. Fixing line endings and hardening the Dockerfile.

[REDACTED]

input.contents:
#!/bin/sh
set -e
echo "Running migrations..."
alembic upgrade head
echo "Migrations complete. Starting uvicorn..."
exec uvicorn backtrace_agent.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

input.new_string:
COPY --chown=agent:agent entrypoint.sh /app/entrypoint.sh

# Strip Windows CRLF so the shebang resolves to /bin/sh (not /bin/sh\r).
RUN sed -i 's/\r$//' /app/entrypoint.sh && chmod +x /app/entrypoint.sh

input.command: cd "e:\Trilogy\Backtrace_TI"; docker compose build agent; docker compose up -d agent; Start-Sleep -Seconds 8; docker compose logs agent --tail 15
input.description: Rebuild agent image and verify startup logs
Verify health endpoint responds

content.text:
## Why this happened

The message `exec /app/entrypoint.sh: no such file or directory` is misleading. The file **was** in the image, but it was saved with **Windows CRLF line endings** (`\r\n`).

On Linux, the shebang becomes `#!/bin/sh\r`, so the kernel looks for an interpreter at `/bin/sh\r`, which does not exist. That shows up as “no such file or directory,” not as a line-ending error.

This often happens when `entrypoint.sh` is created or edited on Windows.

## What we fixed

1. **`entrypoint.sh`** — normalized to Unix (LF) line endings  
2. **`Dockerfile`** — added `sed -i 's/\r$//'` during the image build so CRLF is stripped even if the file is edited on Windows again  
3. **`.gitattributes`** — `*.sh text eol=lf` so Git keeps shell scripts as LF

After rebuild, the agent starts correctly: migrations run, then uvicorn.

## What you should run

```powershell
cd E:\Trilogy\Backtrace_TI
docker compose up --build -d
docker compose logs -f agent
```

You should see:

```text
Running migrations...
Migrations complete. Starting uvicorn...
```

Then check health:

```powershell
Invoke-RestMethod http://localhost:8000/healthz
```

[REDACTED]
